Unnamed: 2: Tourism departments currently operate WITHOUT predictive insights during peak seasons, causing poor management of transport, cleanliness, safety, and resources — degrading the tourist experience.
Use this historical footfall dataset to train ML forecasting models that predict visitor volumes 4–8 weeks ahead, enabling proactive resource allocation.
Seasonal ARIMA with external regressors (festival calendar, weather). Best for monthly aggregate forecasting per spot. Use 2015–2022 as training, 2023–2025 as test.
Handles seasonality + holiday effects natively. Ideal for non-technical tourism boards. Outputs confidence intervals useful for capacity planning.
Long Short-Term Memory for capturing complex temporal dependencies across spots and states. Requires at least 5 years of data — this dataset qualifies.
Gradient boosting for tabular data. Add features: month, state, infra level, weather, festival flag. Strong baseline model for quick deployment.
If predicted visitors for Varanasi in October > 50,000, trigger extra UPSRTC buses and additional train coaches from Mughal Sarai junction 2 weeks in advance.
Forecast peak days at Taj Mahal to pre-position e-rickshaws, regulate private vehicle entry (NGT compliance), and activate overflow parking 3 km away.
For Goa (Dec–Jan), Leh, Shimla: alert airport operators 6 weeks ahead to coordinate charter slots and extra security lanes.
Scale sanitation staff proportional to forecast visitors. At Haridwar Ghats during Kumbh: deploy 3x standard crew starting 10 days before predicted peak.
Forecast monthly waste generation = f(visitors × waste_per_visitor). Route optimization for collection vehicles using predictions 7 days ahead.
For outdoor/festival sites (Pushkar, Kumbh, Thrissur Pooram): pre-position SULABH units based on forecast crowd size.

Unnamed: 1: USE CASE 3 — PUBLIC SAFETY & CROWD CONTROL

Unnamed: 2: Build a composite index: forecast_visitors / venue_capacity. If index > 0.85, trigger crowd management protocol (barriers, alternate entry/exit, CCTV monitoring).
Share footfall forecast with district police 30 days before Navratri/Diwali/Kumbh. Enables advance deployment, patrolling routes, and inter-district coordination.
Pre-position ambulances and first-aid posts at Kedarnath, Vaishno Devi, and Char Dham based on season-opening day predictions.
Correlate forecast visitors with registered accommodation capacity per district. Alert tourism board when gap > 20% so hoteliers can open additional properties.
Forecast ticket revenues for ASI monuments, entry fees, and local taxes to enable municipal budget planning.
High-footfall periods at hill stations (Shimla, Manali, Ooty) strain water supply. Footfall forecasts help JNNURM/Smart City teams pre-plan supply augmentation.
Target Mean Absolute Percentage Error < 12% for monthly forecasts, < 20% for weekly forecasts.
Aim for 4-week reliable forecasts for operational planning; 3-month forecasts for strategic resource allocation.
Retrain models quarterly with fresh data from state tourism boards' footfall counting systems (RFID, ticketing, Google Mobility).
